title: C.R. Cali: How to Exhort Legislators Who Support Abolition and Pro-Life Bills
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States endorses Pastor C.R. Cali's strategy for how abolitionists should exhort legislators (like Texas Rep. Bryan Slaton) who file abolition bills but also support incremental pro-life regulation. The piece offers a tactical script of dos and don'ts for emails and calls aimed at giving such lawmakers the courage to stand exclusively on God's Word.
audiences: Grassroots abolitionist activist preparing to contact a legislator
Sympathetic state legislator weighing whether to support pro-life regulation bills
Pastor or church leader discipling members on civic engagement
Movement strategist evaluating how to relate to mixed-record allies
Pro-life voter curious why abolitionists object to incremental bills

questions.question: Why does Free the States believe abolitionists must speak up to legislators who file abolition bills but also vote for pro-life regulations?
questions.audience: Grassroots abolitionist activist preparing to contact a legislator
questions.answer: Because filing abolition bills, while honorable, does not cancel out the harm of supporting incremental measures that prop up the very pro-life politicians who keep abortion legal. The movement must hold even its friends to a biblical standard rather than soften its message to avoid offense.
questions.quote: Filing abolition bills is important to the growth of the movement and we ought to honor those who self-sacrificially do so; but at the same time, it would be disastrous if the movement softened its stance on the immorality and counter-productivity of pro-life incrementalism for the sake of not offending a bill author or supporter.

questions.question: What concrete actions did Bryan Slaton take in 2021 that earned both abolitionist appreciation and concern?
questions.audience: Pro-life voter curious why abolitionists object to incremental bills
questions.answer: Slaton repeatedly filed bills to abolish abortion in regular and special sessions and tried to leverage House rules to force a vote on abolition, but he also backed every abortion regulation bill that came up and publicly celebrated the heartbeat bill, which abolitionists view as compromise.
questions.quote: He filed a bill to abolish abortion in normal session and in each of the special sessions called by Governor Greg Abbott. He motioned to amend House rules so that bridges and roads could not be renamed unless a vote was taken on the abolition of abortion. He made powerful enemies among the Republican establishment by fighting to abolish abortion. But he also supported every abortion regulation bill that was presented, and celebrated the heartbeat bill on Facebook.

questions.question: What posture does C.R. Cali tell abolitionists to take when contacting a mixed-record legislator?
questions.audience: Grassroots abolitionist activist preparing to contact a legislator
questions.answer: Don't lead with what you like or dislike about the legislator. Instead, acknowledge the political pressure they face and pledge to stand with them when they take the right stand, focusing the conversation on calling them to a biblical standard.
questions.quote: Cali highlights the need for abolitionists to call legislators to a Biblical standard and recommends not focusing on what we appreciate or don't appreciate about a legislator, but rather communicate to legislators that you understand the difficult position they're in and will stand with them strongly if they do what is right.

questions.question: Why does Cali advise against thanking a legislator for past abolition bills or criticizing their compromises in these messages?
questions.audience: Movement strategist evaluating how to relate to mixed-record allies
questions.answer: Both praise for prior actions and criticism for compromises distract from the goal of the message, which is to fortify the legislator for the next stand. The strategy keeps the focus on courage going forward, not a balance sheet of past behavior.
questions.quote:
- Thank him for things that he has done in the past such as his abolition bills or rule changes
- Criticize him for his compromise in supporting pro-life healthcare laws
- Criticize him for attending the Texas Right to Life gala
- Threaten withdrawal of support if he doesn't respond rightly to our exhortations

questions.question: What promise should abolitionists make to a legislator who is being pressured by the pro-life establishment?
questions.audience: Sympathetic state legislator weighing whether to support pro-life regulation bills
questions.answer: They should pledge that when establishment attacks come for refusing to vote for pro-life healthcare regulations, the people will stand with that legislator in his principled, biblical stance against abortion.
questions.quote: Most importantly let him know that WHEN these attacks come, we will be there with him standing to support him in his righteous stance and the only consistent biblical and moral stance against abortion

questions.question: What email subject line does Cali recommend, and why does framing matter?
questions.audience: Grassroots abolitionist activist preparing to contact a legislator
questions.answer: Cali instructs activists to use a specific subject line that frames the message as encouragement against unjust pro-life healthcare laws, keeping the tone supportive and the topic narrow so the legislator hears courage rather than condemnation.
questions.quote: Use the subject line in email "Encouragement to make a principled stand against unjust pro-life healthcare laws"

questions.question: What is the explicit purpose of the email campaign according to Cali?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader discipling members on civic engagement
questions.answer: The email's job is to supply the courage a legislator needs to be the lone voice standing on God's Word against pro-life healthcare laws by promising the unwavering support of the people, and the writer must stay on that single subject.
questions.quote: The purpose of this email to give him the courage he needs to be the lone legislator to stand firmly on God's Word against these pro-life healthcare laws by giving him the unwavering support of the people. Stay focused on that subject matter.

questions.question: Does Free the States want this strategy applied only to Bryan Slaton, or more broadly?
questions.audience: Movement strategist evaluating how to relate to mixed-record allies
questions.answer: Free the States explicitly endorses Cali's approach and urges abolitionists nationwide to apply it whenever a legislator supports both abolition bills and incremental pro-life bills.
questions.quote: Free the States endorses Cali's position and encourages abolitionists all around the country to reach out to legislators who support both incremental and abolition bills with exhortations along these lines.

related.title: State Rep. Bryan Slaton Files Bill to Immediately Abolish Abortion in Texas
related.reason: Background on Slaton's regular-session abolition bill that the article praises while critiquing his other compromises.

related.title: State Rep. Bryan Slaton Refiles Texas Abolition Bill in Special Session
related.reason: Documents the special-session refilings the article cites as evidence of Slaton's abolitionist effort.

related.title: Abolitionist Perspective on the Texas Heartbeat Bill: Three Quick Observations
related.reason: Explains why abolitionists oppose the Texas heartbeat bill that Slaton celebrated, the very compromise this exhortation campaign targets.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Provides the underlying argument for why abolitionists urge legislators to refuse incremental pro-life regulation bills.

related.title: Tennessee Fetal Heartbeat Bill—A Step in the Wrong Direction
related.reason: Companion case study showing the abolitionist critique of heartbeat-style legislation referenced in the article.

related.title: Peeling Back the Texas Pro-Life Façade
related.reason: Context on the Texas pro-life establishment whose pressure on Slaton motivates the exhortation strategy.

related.title: Disgraceful! Pro-Life Establishment Lies About Abolitionist Candidate Warren Hamilton
related.reason: Illustrates the kind of establishment attacks abolitionists are promising to defend principled legislators against.
